frame_analyses.description: Four circular targets visible on white fabric with purple dotted marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase, no instruments visible yet

frame_analyses.description: Left instrument grasping needle with suture attached
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle loading with proper instrument positioning

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned for first bite through fabric at upper target
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle oriented properly for perpendicular tissue entry

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pulled through fabric creating loop pattern
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good suture management, no tangling observed

frame_analyses.description: First throw being constructed with both instruments manipulating suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw being created, appears to be surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: First throw being tightened down to tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tension application, knot appears secure

frame_analyses.description: Second throw construction underway with left hand leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching observed, proper technique for alternating throws

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being constructed with right hand leading
Final throw being tightened and secured
Scissors cutting suture tails close to knot

knot_assessments.notes: Double throw surgeon's knot properly constructed
Single throw with proper hand switching
Final securing throw with good technique

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Good tissue approximation visible, no gaps between fabric edges

technique_summary: Excellent intracorporeal knot tying technique with proper surgeon's knot, consistent hand alternation, and efficient completion time. Good tissue approximation achieved.
